_meta.keyKnowledge: We learn strategies for both general and specific learning contexts, including identifying and organising key ideas.

_source.w: We learn strategies for both general and specific learning contexts, including identifying and organising key ideas.
y: Having a range of learning strategies means we are prepared for any kind of learning challenge.
Knowing how to identify and organise key ideas improves our comprehension and retention.
Adapting our strategies to suit different contexts makes our learning more efficient.
eg: We use summarising to identify key ideas in a text or lesson.
We create graphic organisers to show how ideas are connected.
We choose different strategies for different subjects — visualising for science, note-taking for history.
We reflect on which strategies work best for us in different contexts.
We teach a learning strategy to a classmate and explain when to use it.
We adapt a general strategy for a specific task and evaluate how well it worked.
